payload.query: Hi there, can you recommend some papers on RAG?

payload.text: Hi there, can you recommend some papers on RAG?

top_papers.title: Large Multi-Modal Models (LMMs) as Universal Foundation Models for AI-Native Wireless Systems
top_papers.apa_citation: Xu, S., Thomas, C. K., Hashash, O., Muralidhar, N., Saad, W., & Ramakrishnan, N. (2024). Large Multi-Modal Models (LMMs) as Universal Foundation Models for AI-Native Wireless Systems. *arXiv*. https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.01748

top_papers.title: Ingest-And-Ground: Dispelling Hallucinations from Continually-Pretrained LLMs with RAG
top_papers.apa_citation: Fang, C., Larson, D., Zhu, S., Zeng, S., Summer, W., Peng, Y., Hulovatyy, Y., Rao, R., Forgues, G., Pudota, A., Goncalves, A., & Robert, H. (2024). Ingest-And-Ground: Dispelling Hallucinations from Continually-Pretrained LLMs with RAG. *arXiv*. https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.02825

top_papers.title: Prompt Tuning for Natural Language to SQL with Embedding Fine-Tuning and RAG
top_papers.apa_citation: Jang, J., Bui, T., Choi, Y., & Li, W. (2025). Prompt Tuning for Natural Language to SQL with Embedding Fine-Tuning and RAG. *arXiv*. https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.08245

top_papers.title: MemArchitect: A Policy Driven Memory Governance Layer
top_papers.apa_citation: Kumar, L. S., Ba, Y., & Pan, R. (2026). MemArchitect: A Policy Driven Memory Governance Layer. *arXiv*. https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18330

top_papers.title: MultiHop-RAG: Benchmarking Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Multi-Hop Queries
top_papers.apa_citation: Tang, Y., & Yang, Y. (2024). MultiHop-RAG: Benchmarking Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Multi-Hop Queries. *arXiv*. https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.15391

recommended_papers.title: Large Multi-Modal Models (LMMs) as Universal Foundation Models for AI-Native Wireless Systems
recommended_papers.apa_citation: Xu, S., Thomas, C. K., Hashash, O., Muralidhar, N., Saad, W., & Ramakrishnan, N. (2024). Large Multi-Modal Models (LMMs) as Universal Foundation Models for AI-Native Wireless Systems. *arXiv*. https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.01748
recommended_papers.summary: This paper proposes large multi-modal models (LMMs) as universal foundation models for AI-native wireless systems, emphasizing the use of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for grounding physical symbol representations in real-world wireless systems.

recommended_papers.title: Ingest-And-Ground: Dispelling Hallucinations from Continually-Pretrained LLMs with RAG
recommended_papers.apa_citation: Fang, C., Larson, D., Zhu, S., Zeng, S., Summer, W., Peng, Y., Hulovatyy, Y., Rao, R., Forgues, G., Pudota, A., Goncalves, A., & Robert, H. (2024). Ingest-And-Ground: Dispelling Hallucinations from Continually-Pretrained LLMs with RAG. *arXiv*. https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.02825
recommended_papers.summary: The paper introduces methods to reduce hallucinations in continually-pretrained LLMs by integrating a semantic RAG layer, which grounds responses with factual information to improve accuracy in privacy-related queries.

recommended_papers.title: Prompt Tuning for Natural Language to SQL with Embedding Fine-Tuning and RAG
recommended_papers.apa_citation: Jang, J., Bui, T., Choi, Y., & Li, W. (2025). Prompt Tuning for Natural Language to SQL with Embedding Fine-Tuning and RAG. *arXiv*. https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.08245
recommended_papers.summary: This work presents a framework for error correction in natural language to SQL translation using prompt tuning, embedding fine-tuning, and RAG, achieving a 12% accuracy improvement over existing baselines.

recommended_papers.title: MemArchitect: A Policy Driven Memory Governance Layer
recommended_papers.apa_citation: Kumar, L. S., Ba, Y., & Pan, R. (2026). MemArchitect: A Policy Driven Memory Governance Layer. *arXiv*. https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18330
recommended_papers.summary: MemArchitect is introduced as a governance layer for memory management in LLM agents, addressing contradictions, privacy, and outdated information through rule-based policies and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

recommended_papers.title: MultiHop-RAG: Benchmarking Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Multi-Hop Queries
recommended_papers.apa_citation: Tang, Y., & Yang, Y. (2024). MultiHop-RAG: Benchmarking Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Multi-Hop Queries. *arXiv*. https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.15391
recommended_papers.summary: MultiHop-RAG is a novel dataset and benchmark for evaluating RAG systems on multi-hop queries, highlighting the limitations of existing methods in retrieving and reasoning over multiple pieces of evidence.
